mnemonics: The Russian Doll Node: Like a doll that contains another doll of the same kind, a self-referential structure contains a pointer to another structure of its own type.
keyPoints: A self-referential structure is one that contains a pointer to a structure of the same type.
This is the fundamental building block for linked data structures like linked lists, trees, and graphs.
The structure is defined with at least one member that is a pointer to the structure type itself (e.g., `struct node *next;`).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the self-referential pointer to NULL in the last node, leading to undefined behavior when traversed.
Incorrectly calculating the size of the structure, as the pointer's size is fixed and does not cause infinite recursion.
Creating a circular reference by mistake, which can cause infinite loops during traversal if not handled properly.
